entry.title: it is possible to create a purchase order using a division that does not belong to the company code
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, it is possible to create a purchase order using a division that does not belong to the specified company code.

Consider : 

Organizationnal structure

company code S001-division 0001

company code S002-division 0002

My case is : I create a PO header with Company code S001 item with division 0001, I complete the item.

Then i change the company code to S002. 

My PO can be saved without any error/warning message.

I did it in app : 'manage purchase orders' -F0842A and 'Create purchase order-advanced'-ME21N

same result

then I change the company code

 

I show this is blocking when changing plant despite of the company code, there is a correct error message:

entry.title: Build Partner Add-on with IDE Action
entry.content:
Disclaimer: The namespace /ITAPC3/ is used throughout this document as an example namespace only. Replace it with your own namespace in real implementations. The coding style/naming conventions used in this blog are not recommendations on naming/design 
  
This blog post covers a step-by-step guide to how partners implement IDE Actions as Partner Add-ons in the SAP BTP ABAP Environment. This enables SAP partners to deliver customized tools in the form of custom action in ABAP Development Tools that increase developer productivity. By combining IDE Actions with the installable add-on option of the BTP ABAP Environment, partners have a potentially monetizable option to extend the ABAP Developer tools. It covers the key concepts and technologies – how to extend ABAP Development Tools with IDE Actions and how to leverage the SAP Business Technology Platform ABAP Environment alongside the Landscape Portal to deliver installable add-on products. 

Here we will look into a partner persona, where they can provide the business solution to their consumer with an installable add-on by providing certain action in the ABAP development tools, and also look into the consumer side of how they can consume the solution which caters to the needs of their developer using ABAP development tools (ADT). 

Why IDE actions and Installable add-ons? 

By combining IDE actions and installable add-ons, partners who have been extending software can now also extend the ABAP Development Tools (ADT). 

  • The IDE action framework allows extending the ABAP development tools (ADT) with custom server-driven context-aware actions. IDE Actions allow partners to automate repetitive tasks, such as creating specialized development objects, enhancing code analysis, or automating some code generation within ABAP Development Tools (ADT) for Eclipse. The implementation is completely server-driven using ABAP only. You can also use these custom functionalities with AI integration and XCO libraries. 
  • Installable add-ons are a set of software components bundled together as an add-on by a partner with the intent of providing a solution to their customers.  
  • The Landscape Portal acts as a central tool to allow service providers to perform administration tasks such as hibernating systems, creating test tenants and support users, selecting systems for a pre-upgrade, but also lifecycle management operations such as building, deploying and updating product versions, and even guides you through the configuration and deployment of multitenant SaaS solutions. 

 Business scenario: 

The developer can document development objects in the Knowledge Transfer Document Editor using Markdown syntax. Here partner is offering a translation action with the help of IDE Action as Add-on for the Knowledge Transfer Document and can use the ABAP AI SDK powered by Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) to translate the Markdown syntax and display the output of translated Knowledge Transfer Document. Consumer (The partner's customer) can consume this solution from the partner through landscape portal as Installable add-on and can use customized action in ABAP Development Tools for their development scenario. 

Prerequisites: (Partner) 

  • You’ve purchased entitlements that are necessary for the account setup. See Prepare 
  • You’ve registered a namespace at SAP, for example /NAMESPC/. See Register a Namespace 
  • You’ve registered your add-on at SAP, for example /NAMESPC/PRODUCTX. See Register a Product 
  • You are subscribed to the Landscape Portal 
  • You have a user with the Landscape Portal Admin role collection assigned 
  • Install ABAP development tools for Eclipse (ADT) on your computer. If you already have ADT, make sure it's updated to the latest version. For more information, see ABAP Development Tools. 
  • You need to have access to an SAP BTP ABAP Environment Instance 
  • Business Catalog SAP_A4C_BC_DEV_AIA_PC required for creating and editing IDE actions 

 Prerequisites: (Consumer) 

  • The Landscape Portal Application has been subscribed to and your user has the landscape portal admin role collection assigned 
  • Install ABAP development tools for Eclipse (ADT) on your computer. If you already have ADT, make sure it's updated to the latest version. For more information, see ABAP Development Tools. 

 Steps to Implement as a Partner 

1. Create Software Components 

  • To open the "Manage Software Components" application, log in to your SAP BTP ABAP Environment Fiori Launchpad as an administrator and Search for "Manage Software Components" in the search bar. 
  • use the “Create” button in the “Manage Software Components” app to open the dialog and fill in the information. We enter the name of the software component and a description. In the namespace dropdown you can use your reserved namespace. (Disclaimer: /ITAPC3/ being an example namespace used in this blog) 

  • You clone the software component into the development system. When cloning, the main branch is checked out for the system, a package is created in the system that has the same name as the software component and is also assigned to it. At the same time, you can also set how the clone of the repository behaves in the system (push/pull or just pull). 
  • The software component with the selected branch is now imported into your current system instance. The Clone button turns into a Pull button. From now on, you can use it to pull (remotely available) changes of your software component to your system. Now you can see package under the selected software component in your system. 

2.  Development (Intelligent Scenario) 

Here in this scenario, we would be using ABAP AI Capabilities is our IDE Action implementation class to translate the markdown content into the target language with the help of ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM. To learn more about Developing AI Enabled Applications with ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM, refer to the blog post. 

2.1 Create ISLM Scenario: 

  • Select your package, right-click, New → Other → Intelligent Scenario → /ITAPC3/ADT_INT_SCE 

Enter a name and description. Select SIDEBYSIDE for Scenario Technology field. Choose Next and then click Finish. 

2.2 Create ISLM Model: 

  • Select your package, right-click, New → Other → Intelligent Scenario Model 
  • Fill the Description and Model Name fields. Assign the previously created scenario in Intelligent Scenario Name. Choose Next.  

  • Enter the ExecutableID and Large Language Model Name and Large Language Model Version and Activate the object. 

3.  Development (IDE Action) 

In this section we will look at the IDE action which translates the markdown content into the selected language with the help of intelligent scenario created in the previous step. 

Partners can utilize the ABAP AI SDK powered by ISLM to embed Generative AI features in IDE action implementation class. 

  • From ABAP Development Tools, locate and add to the favorite package “/ITAPC3/IDE_ACTION_EXTENSIONS“ that was created during Create Software Components process. The package has the same name as the software component. 
  • Create a new IDE action either from the main menu, project explorer, or toolbar. 
  • In the main menu, choose File->New->Other... and select IDE Action. The creation wizard opens. 
  • In the creation wizard, enter the project and package details if they are not already filled. Also, provide a name and description for the IDE action. 
  • Choose Next, assign a transport request if needed and Choose Finish. 

  • After we have created the action in the system, we then fill the form with the relevant information to create the action and the Input UI Configuration Class. In the Filter: Cardinality section, decide the number of focused resources that can be selected when running an IDE action. If you want to use your IDE action for any object type, add *ALL as value for the object type. Save and activate your IDE action. 

 

3.1 Defining UI 

  • In the first step we need an action dialog to enable the user to enter data. To do this we can create a new class using the link in the IDE Action. This implements the interface IF_AIA_SD_ACTION_INPUT, but only the method CREATE_INPUT_CONFIG is defined here; the other three methods can be implemented optionally. We define a public type in the class as the structure for the input; we can give the fields certain properties using the ABAP Docs. A list of properties can be found in the documentation, with certain properties being set via configuration. 

 

CLASS /itapc3/cl_trans_ui_info DEFINITION 
  PUBLIC 
  FINAL 
  CREATE PUBLIC . 
  PUBLIC SECTION. 
  INTERFACES if_aia_sd_action_input. 
     "! <p class="shorttext">Simple Controls</p> 
      BEGIN OF ty_input, 
      "! <p class="shorttext">Select the language</p> 
      custom_value_help_text_field TYPE string, 
      "! <p class="shorttext">Enter the markdown source for the translation</p> 
        multiline_text_field         TYPE string, 
      END OF ty_input. 
  PROTECTED SECTION. 
  PRIVATE SECTION. 
ENDCLASS. 

CLASS /ITAPC3/CL_TRANS_UI_INFO IMPLEMENTATION. 
  METHOD if_aia_sd_action_input~create_input_config. 
  " Create the input data with default values 
  DATA(input_data) = VALUE ty_input( ) ##NO_TEXT. 
  " Create the configuration via the factory 
  DATA(configuration) = ui_information_factory>get_configuration_factory( 
                                       )->create_for_data( input_data ). 
  " Set the layout 
  configuration->set_layout( if_sd_config_element=>layout-grid ). 
  " Set configuration for elements 
  “To enable a custom value help for an element, the corresponding configuration must be set via the API: 
   configuration->get_element( 'custom_value_help_text_field' )->set_values( if_sd_config_element=>values_kind-domain_specific_named_items ). 
    configuration->get_element( 'multiline_text_field' )->set_multiline( if_sd_config_element=>height-large ). 
  " Create and return the UI information 
 result = ui_information_factory->for_abap_type( abap_type     = input_data 
             configuration = configuration ). 
 ENDMETHOD. 
 METHOD if_aia_sd_action_input~get_value_help_provider. 
  " Return a reference to your value help handler via the value help provider 
   result = cl_sd_value_help_provider=>create( NEW /ITAPC3/CL_TRAN_VALUE_HELP( ) ). 
  ENDMETHOD. 
ENDCLASS.

 

3.2 Implement a Custom Value Help: 

  • To implement a custom value help that returns domain-specific named items, a handler class must be provided that implements the if_sd_value_help_dsni interface. The get_value_help_items method returns the values for the value help. The values to be returned are of type if_sd_value_help_dsni=>ty_named_item_list. 

 

CLASS /itapc3/cl_trans_ide_action DEFINITION 
  PUBLIC 
  FINAL 
  CREATE PUBLIC. 
  PUBLIC SECTION. 
  INTERFACES if_aia_action. 
  PROTECTED SECTION. 
  PRIVATE SECTION. 
  METHODS get_input_as_html 
      IMPORTING ui_input      TYPE /itapc3/cl_trans_ui_info=>ty_input 
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE string. 
ENDCLASS. 

CLASS /ITAPC3/CL_TRANS_IDE_ACTION IMPLEMENTATION. 
 METHOD get_input_as_html. 
    FINAL(nl) = cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline. 
    TRY. 
        DATA(completion_api) = cl_aic_islm_compl_api_factory=>get( )->create_instance( '/ITAPC3/ADT_INT_SCE' ). 
        DATA(prompt) = |You are a translator. Reject Hate Speech. Translate the given text into { ui_input-custom_value_help_text_field }:{ nl }{ ui_input-multiline_text_field }| ##NO_TEXT. 
        DATA(translation) = completion_api->execute_for_string( prompt )->get_completion( ). 
      CATCH cx_aic_api_factory INTO DATA(aic_api_factory_ex). 
      RETURN. 
      CATCH cx_aic_completion_api INTO DATA(aic_completion_api_ex). 
      RETURN. 
    ENDTRY. 
    result =  '<h3 style="color:gray">Translate to (' && ui_input-custom_value_help_text_field && ')</h3>' && nl  && '<pre>' && translation && '</pre>'. 
  ENDMETHOD. 

  METHOD if_aia_action~run. 
  DATA(focused_resource) = context->get_focused_resource( ). 
    TRY. 
        DATA ui_input TYPE /itapc3/cl_trans_ui_info=>ty_input. 
        context->get_input_config_content( )->get_as_structure( IMPORTING result = ui_input ). 
      CATCH cx_sd_invalid_data INTO DATA(exception). 
        CLEAR ui_input. 
    ENDTRY. 
    DATA(popup_result) = cl_aia_result_factory=>create_html_popup_result( ). 
    popup_result->set_content( get_input_as_html( ui_input ) ). 
    result = popup_result. 
  ENDMETHOD. 
ENDCLASS.

 

To start the action in Eclipse, we can copy the markdown source from any KTD Editor and use  use the shortcut Ctrl + Alt + R for Windows or CMD + OPT + R for Mac or choose Run->ABAP IDE Action... from the main menu to start the Action Dialog, where our IDE Action should now be available. 

Once all the required objects are created and the action is working as per our expectation, we have to release the Transport Request. 

4.  Create Add-on Product using Landscape Portal 

The Landscape Portal acts as a central tool to allow service providers to perform administration tasks such as hibernating systems, creating test tenants and support users, selecting systems for a pre-upgrade, but also lifecycle management operations such as building, deploying and updating product versions, and even guides you through the configuration and deployment of multitenant SaaS solutions. 

Here partner can create a new Add-on product, register your product, build a product version via pipelines and deploy a product version. 

4.1 Register Product 

A product refers to an ABAP add-on that can be installed on SAP BTP, ABAP environment. 

  • Log into the Landscape Portal. For more information, see Landscape Portal   
  • Click on the Register Product tile to open the app. 
  • A list of all your created products, including their product ID, namespace and registration status, is displayed. The registration status indicates whether a product has already been registered to the global account you are currently logged in to. 
  • Click the Create button in the top right corner. 
  • Enter the name of the product you want to create and confirm with Create. Your request for creation has been sent and the registration status of the product changes to “Pending…”. The namespace is automatically determined from the product name you entered, and the product ID is generated as part of the creation process. 

 4.2 Build Product Version 

You can use the Build Product Version app to easily trigger the build of new product versions via pipelines based on templates that you can configure for different use cases such as new (test or production) release deliveries, (test or production) support packages or (test or production) hotfixes/patch deliveries. 

  • Log in to the Landscape Portal and click on the tile Build Product Version. For more information, see Build Product Version 
  • Select one of your products from the list. You will be forwarded to the Product Versions tab. Here, you can see a table listing all the existing versions of this product, their type, when they were created, as well as their delivery status. 
  • You also need to Configure a Pipeline Template for release build. For more information, see Configure a Pipeline Template and Create a template for release build.  
  • To create a new product version, click the Create button, choose Release Delivery and select the desired type. The resulting product version will be calculated automatically. 
  • Select software components to be included in your new product version. To add additional software components, click the Add button on the right. For each software component, define a component version, enter the desired branch and commit ID as seen in the Manage Software Components app, and select in which languages your product version should be built. To get relevant commit ID for the software component involved in the build, you can refer the help page to get more details on this. Identify the commit based on which you wish to build your add-on product. 

  • Click the Build Product Version button to trigger the build of the pipeline for your new product version. 
  • You will be redirected to the Pipeline Status screen, where you can track the progress of your build. 

4.3 Check Product Version 

The Check Product Version app lets you view the results of product version delivery infrastructure checks to see if your product version is ready for delivery.  

  • Click on the Check Product Version tile to open the app. For more information, see Check Product Version 
  • A list of all your products is displayed. Select one of the products from the list. 
  • Ready for production (P): The product version is ready for production use. 

4.4 Publish Product 

You, as a partner, have developed a product that you want to make available to your customers as an add-on that they can install into their system. You can use the Publish Product app to publish your product in a specific version for use by customers. Your customer can then use the Discover Product app to view all product versions that were published, i.e. made available, for them and can register a product, thus adding a product to their pool of product versions available for deployment. 

  1. Log into the Landscape Portal. 
  1. Click the Publish Product tile to open the app. For more information, see Publish Product 
  1. Select the product from the list. You are forwarded to the configuration page. Click the Edit button. 
  1. In the Product Configuration section, select the Product Version that you want to publish and choose SAP Store as the Distribution Channel. 
  1. Click the Publish button to publish your product version 

 

Steps performed from the Consumer 

1. Discover Product 

  • Once Partner has made their product available and has published a product version using the Publish Product app, customers can register the product via Discover Product app. 
  • After registrations customers can deploy the product using Deploy Product.  

2.  Deploy Product 

You can use the Deploy Product app to deploy or update your product version in specific systems. 

  • Sign in to the Landscape Portal. 
  • Click on the Deploy Product tile to open the app. 
  • On the left side, under Product and Product Version Selection, select which product and version you want to deploy or update from the drop-down menu. 
  • In the Available Systems section, select at least one system in which you want to deploy or update the product version. You can also select multiple systems at once. 
  • Click the Deploy button. A dialog box will open. Fill in the remaining details: 
  • Title: Choose a title for your update. 
  • From: Select the time the update can begin. 
  • To: The estimated time at which the product version update will be completed. 
  • Confirm with Schedule. 
  • Check the list under Product Version Update Status. Here, all update requests as well as their current status and progress are displayed. The update you just scheduled has been added to the list. 
  • Once the product is deployed successfully then Customer can access the product in target system. 

SAP BTP automatically activates the software component provided from the partner in the ABAP environment, makes packages visible and registers IDE extensions. 

3.  Test IDE Action 

Create/Use ABAP Cloud project in the ABAP instance where you have deployed the product.This should be a development system. 

Open any Knowledge Transfer Document in the editor and copy the content. 

To open the Run ABAP IDE Action dialog, use the shortcut Ctrl + Alt + R for Windows or CMD + OPT + R for Mac or choose Run->ABAP IDE Action... from the main menu. 

Select the IDE action “Translate markdown content” you would like to run Select Run. 

It will open the action input dialog where you can select the target language you want to translate and in next text box area provide the Knowledge Transfer Document content. 

Select Run. 

The result dialog for the selected IDE action opens the result dialog where it translates the Knowledge Transfer Document to the selected language option from the input dialog.

entry.title: Re: Custom Attributes from Job Role not appearing in Job...
entry.content:
Dear @Athiseshan16 

Good question.

Unfortunately, it is not possible to add custom content in the Job Profile Template. The mapped custom attributes that you can set up on the Job Role will not display in Competency/Skill section in the Job Profile since these are different attribute types. 

There's a KBA that confirms the Job Profile templates only support some default sections 

2560641 - Unable to add Custom Content types in the Job Profile Template
https://me.sap.com/notes/2560641/E

There's an enhancement request available to vote/comment and refers to this topic:

Customized fields/sections in job profile template
 https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/355800/

Hope this will help to clarify, 

Regards, 

Jordi

entry.title: Putaway and Stock Removal Strategies in SAP Logistics Management
entry.content:
A question that we have been asked multiple times: which putaway and stock removal strategies does SAP Logistics Management currently support? And where do I find the relevant configuration / setup?

In this blog post, I would like to share some insights into the current system behaviour: 

The answer to the question is short: SAP Logistics Management currently supports the fixed bin strategy combined with strict FIFO (first in first out) in the outbound process, in inbound it is fixed bin. So what does this mean precisely for putaway and stock removal and how to set it up?

 

Inbound - Putaway Strategy

In the inbound process, when creating a putaway warehouse task, the logistics clerk has two options: manual or automatic task creation.

While in the manual task creation, the logistics clerk can manually enter the destination bin (or leave it empty), the automatic task creation follows this logic:

  1. It checks for a fixed bin assignment of the product. In case this exists, then the fixed bin is taken as destination bin. 
  2. In case it does not find a fixed bin, the destination bin of the task is left empty and has to be entered upon task confirmation by the warehouse operative. 

Both manual and automatic task creation work without fixed bin assignments. 

 

Outbound - Stock Removal Strategy

In the outbound process, the logistics clerk also has two options: manual and automatic warehouse task creation.

Same as in the inbound process, the logistics clerk can manually select the source storage bin during manual task creation: 

The search help on the source storage bin shows a list of unassigned stock of stock type "unrestricted usage":

The second option, automatic task creation, follows this logic: 

  1. It checks for a fixed bin assignment of the product. In case this exists, then the stock is allocated from the fixed bin. 
  2. In case there are multiple or no fixed bins assigned to the product, it follows strict FIFO. 

Also in the outbound process, both manual and automatic task creation work without fixed bin assignments. This enables you to run your processes without setting up anything here and fine-tuning them as you go (the "grow as you go" principle we are following with SAP Logistics Management). 

 

Setup of Fixed bin assignment

The assignment of products to fixed bins happens via the app Manage Fixed Bin Assignments which is part of the app Manage Warehouse Layout :

In this app you can assign one or multiple products to one or multiple fixed bins. 

 

Configure Staging and Goods Receipt bin

There is one prerequisite for the automatic warehouse task creation which is the maintenance of the goods receipt bin in inbound and the staging bin in outbound. You can either enter this manually or configure a default goods receipt and staging bin per warehouse in order to increase the level of automation here.  This configuration happens via the app configure warehouse processes:

 

Conclusion

Even without any configuration or setup, the system will create putaway and stock removal warehouse tasks with the create task automatically functionality. In case you are working with fixed bins, this has to be setup in the above mentioned app (stay tuned for the upload of fixed bin assignments). 

You may argue now: how about other strategies such as FEFO (first expired first out) or empty bin allocation during putaway? The above mentioned is just the beginning so stay tuned and watch out for more and if you like, comment this blog post with the three most relevant putaway and stock removal strategies that you think we should address next.

 

Want to learn more about SAP Logistics Management?

Explore the SAP Logistics Management Online Course 

Visit the SAP Logistics Management Product Page

Have a look at our Roadmap

entry.title: Re: Resource Handover for Product WTs in S/4HANA Cloud Pu...
entry.content:
Hello @AdamT89 

Thank you for your questions.

First, I will directly answer your questions then, provide some further details.

Question 1: What is the standard procedure to release the resource assignment after the first step (source confirmation) so a second resource can take over and confirm the destination? (I have even tried in Warehouse Monitor to unassign the old resource and then assign a new one, but it still says that the old res. is processing the task)

Answer: For your exact design, there is no standard same WT handover procedure that I am aware of. The standard approach is to design the movement with LOSC, an intermediate step, or two separate tasks.

Question 2: Is there a standard Exception Code available in Public Cloud to trigger this handover for Product WTs?

Answer: I am not aware of any for that in Public Cloud. There is a general exception code but not a standard code for same WT resource handover/stock on resource transfer.

Question 3: How can the "Stock on Resource" be transferred from Resource A to Resource B within the same Warehouse Task?

Answer: There is no Public Cloud function for doing that within the same product WT. The supported design is to use LOSC, intermediate bins, two WTs or HUs to create a process.

Here are some details for your questions.


The reason behind the unavailability of handover inside the same product based 2 step warehouse tasks after source confirmation is that SAP’s standard resource logic is still source bound at warehouse order level. Therefore, before you can confirm product warehouse tasks, the warehouse order must be assigned to the resource you are currently logged on to. Once Resource A has started and stock is effectively on that resource, Resource B cannot simply continue the same task as if the stock had been transferred.

Consult these references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c46da892efa14cea8347e9b872199d6f/07bcf774644344b8a80c943978791ba6.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/c46da892efa14cea8347e9b872199d6f/de6f70f9c74d414a93af3c3825005772.html?locale=en-US

Your Warehouse monitor unassign and assign attempt therefore does not solve the real issue. It may change warehouse order assignment, but it does not create a documented standard transfer of the in-process stock from Resource A to Resource B within the same product WT. This also highlights why you got that error behaviour.

Regarding standard procedure to hand over after source confirmation, the Public Cloud handles modelling a physical handover point by introducing a process break, not to continue the same WT with a second source. A supported capability within the Public Cloud is layout-oriented storage control with intermediate bins. So, that is the standard design if you need one person to bring stock to an intermediate point and another person to continue from there. So, for a two building replenishment process, the standard design options are:

  • Use LOSC with intermediate bin
  • Or redesign the movement into two separate warehouse tasks
  • Or use handling units if your business process allow the stock to be handed over as an HU rather than as loose product stock.

Regarding exception codes, see the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/e8a30c35c57f4391a22aa6102fa3be24.html?locale=en-US

Regarding how stock on resource can be transferred from Resource A to Resource B within the same WT, I would term that a current design gap. You may raise this to SAP to influence product enhancement.

However, there is no standard modelled handover object between building. Practically, the process has no formal transfer point. So, alternatives such as LOSC with intermediate bins, internal warehouse movements, and HU based handling are the supported method in Public Cloud.

For your requirement, the recommended Public Cloud design is:

  • Set up layout-oriented storage control so the first step ends in an intermediate bin near the handover point, and the second step starts from there. That creates a proper split between the two physical execution steps that is properly controlled by the system.
  • If layout-oriented storage controls are not desired, then redesign the replenishment into two separate internal warehouse tasks:
    • Building 1 source bin to handover/intermediate area
    • Handover/intermediate area to Building 2 destination bin
  • If the stock can be physically handed over as a pallet, cart or other load carrier, move it as an HU so the handover is tied to the HU rather than you trying to pass a product WT between resources.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Ask Me Anything with SAP Champions – Monthly Series (Session 2)
entry.content:
Hey dear SAP Community,

My name is Maria Antonieta Lopez,  I currently work as an SAP program director focusing on global SAP transformation programs, SAP S/4HANA implementations, enterprise architecture, governance frameworks, and digital innovation within the SAP ecosystem.

As an SAP Champion myself, I’ll be leading the March edition for the Ask Me Anything series, which we started together with my dear friend Sebastiano Marchesini.

So yes…

You can ask. Me. ANYTHING.

After 20+ years living inside SAP transformations, I’ve probably seen the good, the bad… and the “how did this system survive go-live?” moments. 😅

So over the next weeks I’ll be answering any question you have -- No filters - Just real SAP Live --.

💡We can explore topics like:

⚙️SAP S/4HANA implementations and global transformation programs. - How to successfully deliver end-to-end SAP S/4HANA implementations and large-scale transformation programs, enabling organizations to modernize their core business processes, standardize global operations, and adopt next-generation digital capabilities.

🏗️ SAP Enterprise Architecture. - Processes, artifacts, and strategies for TOGAF-based SAP enterprise architecture.

🧼 Clean Core in SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public). - Project management processes for implementing or creating extensions or interfaces in your project. (BUILD for configurators and its use with Joule, pre-built APIs, newly developed APIs, and, of course, our platform-as-a-service, SAP Integration Suite, obviously within our beloved SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP).

☁️ RISE or GROW with SAP + Clean Core Governance & Methodologies. - From SAP Activate  to frameworks such as SAP Application Extension Methodology, Integration Solution Advisory Methodology, SAP Design Thinking, SAP Data Architecture Framework, and why not… even the SAP Business AI Framework.

We can talk about which format to use, or the process for something, or even… why I have to do this task.... and I'll tell you what will happen if you don't...

Muahaha! …💀 soo Yes… my SAP horror stories - like winter - are coming…

🏛️ How to build an SAP Center of Excellence (CoE) or provide an SAP AM services and actually 💙 live happily ever after with SAP 💙 using Cloud ALM + Focused Build + Activate.

🌍Globalization in a nutshell or by country. To comply locally and succeed globally... Long live SAP Document & Reporting Compliance! And our SAP partners' DRC extensions.

🧭 Solution roadmaps, etc.

And... if I don't know the answer? 👉 I promise I'll find someone who does... for you.

 

We have everything ready...  💬 Now it’s your turn.

Drop your question in the comments section 👇or You can ask anonymously here 👉 AMA Champion Anonymously Questions  or Scanning this QR 

 

This AMA edition will be open for questions until March 27, 2026.

After that, selected questions will be answered in a recorded video published on the SAP Community YouTube channel  👉https://www.youtube.com/@SAPCommunity 
A blog recap summarizing the questions and answers will also be published for the community.

Cuentame…

🤓What SAP topic should we explore together? ESPAÑOL or ENGLISH

entry.title: Getting Started with SAP BTP Kyma Runtime Using the CLI
entry.content:
Introduction

If you're learning SAP BTP Kyma Runtime, most tutorials point you straight to the cockpit UI. But understanding the CLI tools — btp and kubectl — gives you a much deeper understanding of what's actually happening under the hood. In this post I'll walk through connecting to a Kyma cluster and exploring its core components purely from the command line.

Prerequisites

  • SAP BTP Trial account with Kyma Runtime enabled
  • btp CLI installed (download here)
  • kubectl installed

Step 1: Find Your Subaccount and Kyma Instance

Log in to BTP via CLI and find your subaccount:

btp login
btp list accounts/subaccount

Then find your Kyma environment instance:

btp list accounts/environment-instance --subaccount <subaccount-id>

You should see something like:

environment name              environment id       environment type   state
<subdomain>-7tn04i0d          <environment-id>     kyma               OK

Get the details including your kubeconfig URL:

btp get accounts/environment-instance <environment-id> --subaccount <subaccount-id>

This returns a labels field containing your KubeconfigURL and APIServerURL.

Step 2: Download the Kubeconfig and Connect kubectl

Download the kubeconfig from the Kyma Dashboard or via the KubeconfigURL, then point kubectl at it:

cp ~/Downloads/kubeconfig.yaml ~/.kube/kyma-config.yaml
export KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/kyma-config.yaml
kubectl get nodes

On a trial cluster you'll see an AWS EC2 worker node:

NAME                        STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION
<node-name>.ec2.internal    Ready    worker   7d    v1.33.7

Step 3: Explore the Kyma Cluster

Namespaces

kubectl get namespaces

Key namespaces:

Namespace Purpose

kyma-systemCore Kyma components
istio-systemService mesh
defaultYour workloads

Core Components in kyma-system

kubectl get pods -n kyma-system

Pod Purpose

api-gateway-controller-managerManages APIRule resources
btp-manager-controller-managerManages BTP service bindings
istio-controller-managerManages Istio service mesh
sap-btp-operator-controller-managerProvisions BTP services as Kubernetes resources
warden-*Image trust validation

Kyma Custom Resources (CRDs)

kubectl api-resources | grep kyma

Key CRDs:

Resource Purpose

APIRuleExpose services externally with auth/routing
RateLimitThrottle API traffic
BtpOperatorConfigure BTP service operator
KymaTop-level Kyma installation resource

Step 4: Understanding an APIRule

The APIRule is the most important Kyma concept for exposing workloads. Here's what a typical one looks like:

apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v2
kind: APIRule
spec:
  gateway: kyma-system/kyma-gateway
  hosts:
    - myapp.<cluster-id>.kyma.ondemand.com
  rules:
    - methods: [GET, POST]
      noAuth: true
      path: /*
  service:
    name: myapp
    port: 80

Traffic flow:

Internet
  → https://myapp.<cluster-id>.kyma.ondemand.com
  → kyma-gateway (Istio ingress in kyma-system)
  → Service: myapp:80
  → Pod (with Istio sidecar injected automatically)

Note the noAuth: true setting — fine for development but in production you'd replace this with a JWT handler pointing to XSUAA or another identity provider.

Key Observations

  • Istio sidecar injection is automatic — any pod in a labelled namespace gets a second container (2/2 in kubectl get pods)
  • APIRules replace Ingress in Kyma — don't use standard Kubernetes Ingress resources
  • BTP services bind as Kubernetes Secrets — via ServiceInstance and ServiceBinding resources managed by the SAP BTP Operator

Next Steps

  • Bind a BTP service (XSUAA, Destination Service) using ServiceInstance and ServiceBinding
  • Secure an APIRule with JWT authentication
  • Deploy a Kyma Serverless Function

entry.title: A DevOps Day with AI Assistant @LGM
entry.content:
In this blog post, I walk through a real day of DevOps work in LGM with an AI coding assistant by my side.

This post shows how an AI assistant (Claude Code) fits naturally into that routine — helping with tasks like reviewing and approving pull requests, investigating CI/CD build failures, navigating unfamiliar code across repositories, and drafting PR descriptions — all without leaving the terminal.

Rather than replacing the developer, the AI acts as a knowledgeable pair-programming partner that accelerates the repetitive and context-heavy parts of DevOps work, freeing up time for deeper engineering decisions.

 A video walkthrough is included below:

https://video.sap.com/media/t/1_1vuii967

entry.title: Re: Clean Core: architecture challenge or decision-making...
entry.content:
Hi Jean,

I can confirm this completely, even though I have no concrete experience of this in migration projects, I see it every day in day-to-day business.

The "supposedly" easier way of adapting the system to existing processes is often chosen without questioning them. The administrative effort such as documentation, maintenance and updates that is accumulated step by step when specific solutions are developed is usually overlooked. Highly critical key functions are created which can only be handled by people with a high knowledge and long implementation time.

A rigorous allocation of the costs incurred (development, IT admin) to the originator (business) and a cost-efficient controlling of individualization could help to contain this and promote a better understanding of why Clean Core is an very important topic.

Have a nice day

Kind regards Jan

entry.title: Re: Global account cost centre sync issue
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

In this case, I asked the chatbot for support. Here's the response. Can you please check whether it resolves your issue?

It seems that the issue with the tenant creation via URM is due to inconsistent cost center data being received from the CIS service. Here are some steps you can take to resolve this issue:

  1. Verify Event Data:

    • Ensure that all events related to the global account ‘def19557-89f7-4fee-b33e-9c26d1a9acd2’ are consistently showing the correct cost center ‘101039103’.
    • Identify and correct any events that still show the old cost center ‘101830049’.

  2. Check CIS Service Configuration:

    • Review the configuration of the CIS service to ensure it is correctly set up to send the updated cost center information.
    • Check for any caching mechanisms that might be holding onto outdated cost center data.

  3. Coordinate with UCL Team:

    • Work closely with the UCL team to ensure that the events are processed correctly and that the correct cost center is being used in tenant creation.
    • Provide them with the details of the events that contain incorrect data for further investigation.

  4. Manual Override:

    • If the issue persists, consider manually overriding the cost center information in the URM system for the affected global account.

  5. Monitor Future Events:

    • Set up monitoring to ensure that future events consistently reflect the correct cost center.

By following these steps, you should be able to resolve the issue and ensure that the correct cost center is used for tenant creation.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Interactive report screen refresh issue cl_gui_alv_gr...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

In this case, I asked the chatbot for support. Here's the response. Can you please check whether it resolves your issue?

It seems that the issue with the screen not refreshing correctly is related to how the ALV grid is being managed. Here are some steps you can take to resolve this issue:

  1. Ensure Proper Freeing of Objects:

    • Make sure that the ALV grid and container objects are properly freed before creating them again. Uncomment the lines that free these objects:
      CALL METHOD r_grid_9001->free.
CALL METHOD r_container_9001->free.

  2. Recreate Objects on Each Click:

    • Ensure that the objects for the container and grid are recreated each time a new record is clicked. This will help in refreshing the data:
      IF r_container_9001 IS INITIAL.
  CREATE OBJECT r_container_9001
    EXPORTING
      container_name = 'CUST_NEW'.

  CREATE OBJECT r_grid_9001
    EXPORTING
      i_parent = r_container_9001.
ENDIF.

  3. Refresh Table Display:

    • Use the refresh_table_display method to refresh the grid with new data:
      CALL METHOD r_grid_9001->refresh_table_display.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  " Implement suitable error handling here
ENDIF.

  4. Flush GUI Changes:

    • Use cl_gui_cfw=>flush() to ensure that all changes are flushed to the frontend:
      cl_gui_cfw=>flush().

By following these steps, you should be able to resolve the issue and ensure that the correct data is displayed when a new record is clicked.

Kind regards,

 

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Table line items not displaying in duplicated form co...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

In this case, I asked the chatbot for support. Here's the response. Can you please check whether it resolves your issue?

It seems that the issue with the table data not appearing in the duplicate form copy is related to how the data binding and refresh logic are implemented. Here are some steps you can take to resolve this issue:

  1. Check Data Binding:

    • Ensure that the duplicate form’s table is correctly bound to the XML data source. Double-check the binding settings to make sure they match those of the original form.

  2. Use Subform for Repetition:

    • Consider using a subform with the ‘Repeat Subform for Each Data Item’  option enabled. This can help in dynamically replicating table data across multiple copies.

  3. Refresh Logic:

    • Implement logic to refresh the data binding for the duplicate form. You can use scripting to trigger a refresh event or rebind the data when the form is loaded.

  4. Debugging:

    • Use debugging tools to check if the data is being correctly passed to the duplicate form. Ensure that the XML nodes are accessible and correctly mapped.

  5. Consult Documentation:

    • Refer to Adobe LiveCycle Designer documentation for advanced scripting techniques that can help in resolving data binding issues.

By following these steps, you should be able to resolve the issue and ensure that the table data appears dynamically in both copies during preview or print.

For more information, see How to repeat texts in output form template?.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: MODIFY ENTITY FOR CREATE I_SALESORDERTP throws dump i...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

In this case, I asked the chatbot for support. Here's the response. Can you please check whether it resolves your issue?

The issue you’re facing with the runtime error RAISE_SHORTDUMP when scheduling the job through a job template might be related to how the EML statement handles multiple lines in the sales order. Here are some suggestions to resolve this issue:

  1. Check Content ID References:

    • Ensure that all content IDs are correctly referenced and that there are no missing preceding content IDs. This is crucial when dealing with multiple lines in a sales order.

  2. Debugging:

    • Run the job in a test environment with detailed logging enabled to capture more information about the error. This can help identify the exact point of failure.

  3. Review EML Statement:

    • Double-check the EML statement used for creating sales orders with multiple lines. Ensure that the syntax and logic are correct and that all necessary references are included.

  4. Modify Job Template:

    • Consider modifying the job template to handle multiple lines differently, possibly by breaking down the process into smaller steps or batches.

  5. Consult Documentation:

    For more information, see Entity Manipulation Language (EML).

By following these steps, you should be able to resolve the issue and successfully schedule the job for creating sales orders with multiple lines.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Replication in SAP S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

I'm copying the comment to the Answer field because it seems to contain helpful hints.

To summarise, in the public cloud, Replicate by Replication model is monitored through monitoring of replication and application log, not necessarily through Message Dashboard. There are scenarios where Message Dashboard may show integration messages. The fact that it is empty in your case may be due to:

  • Your system did not generate any outbound messages yet
  • The process is still going on in the background, or
  • You may be filtering on the wrong sender, interface, time window or status.

See Replicate by Replication Model, Data Replication and Plants-Replicate for more details.

To better understand what might be wrong since you said, you have set up the communications correctly, I have a few questions:

  1. Can you confirm your replication actually started and ran? Basically saying, did you use the initialisation mode, do you have the Application Log from within the replication that shows you selected records, processed and sent them?
  2. Did you use the Monitor Replication app to check your last run timestamp, status (processing, finished or error log), Counts (selected, sent, failed). See Data Replication for more details.
  3. In your Manage Dashboard, did you validate the filters and authorisations?

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: How can I allow a communication user to schedule a ba...
entry.content: The entire customer base has access to the Ideation Tool “Customer Influence.” Since its rollout around 2014, no customer has submitted a request through this channel describing use cases or scenarios that would require different or extended functionality in this area. In contrast, other topics have gained significant visibility and accumulated the highest number of votes, naturally receiving higher priority and attention. If there is interest in enabling additional approaches or supporting use cases that are currently not achievable with the delivered communication scenario SAP_COM_0326, and which could potentially be integrated into existing capabilities or form the basis for a new integration option, these needs should be raised through the official Customer Influence tool. By making such requests visible and gathering interest from other customers, the topic can gain the necessary traction. With sufficient visibility and voting, this may ultimately lead to new tools, enhanced features, or extended integration scenarios in the future.

entry.title: Re: STO Process Intercompany After Outbound Delivery Dire...
entry.content: Hello Chris, Thanks for your replay. You can also manage transportation charges separately through Transportation Management. Freight orders and freight bookings support: Charge calculation, Freight cost confirmation and posting, Freight invoicing from carrier or service provider invoices. Because you explicitly mentioned MIRO, the process sounds like you expect an intercompany invoice verification step. In Public Cloud, that points to 5HP, and for that scenario the answer is: Outbound delivery: Yes Transport charges: Yes, via TM if it is in scope Direct GR: Yes MIRO without billing: No, because itis not in the standard intercompany process. This is the customer requirement could you please help me for this. It means a lot. Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: Re: CX_SADL_DUMP_APPL_MODEL_ERROR while sending GET Reque...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

In this case, I asked out internal chatbot for support. Here's the response. Can you please check whether it resolves your issue?

Based on the error message you've provided, it seems like there is an issue with the implementation of your query provider class ZCL_SAMPLE_SERVICE.

The error indicates that the system is having trouble creating an instance of this class due to a mismatch between the expected class and the actual one.

Upon looking at your code, it appears that you have inherited from /iwbep/cx_gateway, which is not appropriate for a query provider class. Instead, you should inherit from CL_SADL_ENTITY_EMLENABLD_QP or another suitable superclass.

Here's how you can modify your class definition:

 Copy code

1CLASS ZCL_SAMPLE_SERVICE DEFINITION
2  PUBLIC
3  INHERITING FROM CL_SADL_ENTITY_EMLENABLD_QP
4  FINAL
5  CREATE PUBLIC .
6...
7ENDCLASS.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Alternate Reconciliation Account and Overwriting cont...
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm copying the comment to the Answer field as it seems to provide helpful information.

Currently, there is no standard way to use alternate reconciliation accounts with ERS in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. The MRM_ERS_DATA_Modify_CE BAdI does not include reconciliation account parameters, and forcing overwrite through Open FI is not feasible due to the automated nature of ERS processing. Consider process alternatives or submit enhancement requests for future product development.

Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Is It Time to Bring AI Workflows Into SAP Automation?
entry.content:
You are right that most SAP automation is still rule-based, which works for structured processes but struggles when data patterns change. Adding AI into workflows can help by introducing a decision layer that evaluates context before triggering the next step.

For example, in purchase order workflows AI could detect unusual order values or vendor patterns and route approvals dynamically instead of relying only on fixed rules. It can also help with cross-system integrations by identifying data mismatches before they break the workflow.

From what I have seen, many teams are experimenting with this through SAP BTP services or SAP Integration Suite, while keeping governance inside the SAP workflow layer to maintain auditability.

Curious to see how others here are approaching AI assisted automation in their SAP environments.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Updating Operation wise Confirmation Time...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

Here's what our internal chatbot responded. Can you please check whether the response helps resolve your issue?

To address the issue of updates not reflecting in the system when using EML with I_ProductionOrderConfirmationTP,  consider the following steps:

  1. Check EML Statement Logic: Ensure that the logic for updating both quantity and time is correctly implemented. Verify that the fields OpConfirmedWorkQuantity1 and OpConfirmedWorkQuantity2 are being updated with the correct values.

  2. Review Data Mapping: Confirm that the data mapping in the EML statement is accurate. The %data structure should correctly correspond to the fields you intend to update.

  3. Validate Confirmation Group: Ensure that the confirmationgroup used in the EML statement matches the intended production order confirmation group.

  4. Check for Errors: Utilize the FAILED DATA and REPORTED DATA outputs to identify any errors or issues reported during the EML execution.

  5. Alternative Approach: If the current approach does not work, consider using a different method to update the fields, such as directly interacting with the database or using a different API.

  6. Consult Documentation: Review SAP documentation for any specific requirements or constraints related to updating these fields using EML.

For more information, see APIs for Manufacturing - Read Time Ticket Confirmation.

If these steps do not resolve the issue, further investigation into the system configuration or consulting with SAP support may be necessary.

Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: QR IBAN on Billing Document
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I'm very sorry for the delay.

I asked our internal chatbot for support. Here's the response. Can you please check whether it helps you resolve the issuer?

To address the issue of the incorrect IBAN being printed on the invoice form, consider the following steps:

  1. Check Configuration Settings: Ensure that the configuration settings for QR-bill are correctly set up for both CHF and EUR currencies. This includes verifying that the correct IBAN is associated with each currency.

  2. Review QR-Bill Setup: According to the QR-Bill documentation, make sure that the QR-IBAN and QR reference are correctly configured in the system. The QR-IBAN should be used for payments with a structured reference.

  3. Validate Company Code Configuration: Ensure that the company code is configured for QR-bills and that the currency settings are correctly applied. The QR-bill functionality should be set for both CHF and EUR.

  4. Check IBAN Assignment: Verify that the IBANs are correctly assigned to the house bank information in the posting parameters of the invoice items. This can affect which IBAN is selected during the printing process.

  5. Consult SAP Documentation: Review the SAP documentation Switzerland - QR-Bill for any specific requirements or constraints related to QR-bill configuration and IBAN assignment.

If these steps do not resolve the issue, further investigation into the system configuration or consulting with SAP support may be necessary.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Introducing SAP Integration Suite, enhanced edition
entry.content:
The AI features in this new edition....

Developer Productivity: Text to iflow and script optimization.

It doesnt really make a huge difference to developer productivity as first of all developer productivity has never been a valid business case for an upgrade or a more expensive edition. Second, the demos we have seen so far depict that this is still in its infancy. Third, even if it saves time in building an iflow it would be less than 30 minutes of time saved per iflow.

Any LLM can optimize a script for free in seconds.

API Anomaly Detection: I dont think this is a feature that should need an enhanced edition. Should be standard across all editions.

Reduced number of messages from 10 Million to 500k? This would be a big downgrade if at all its true.

Advanced Event Mesh Included in Integration Suite: This is certainly welcome IF the price hasn't increased astronomically BUT It strictly limits the target customers as not everyone needs Advanced event mesh when Event mesh alone is more than enough for them and would be cautious of paying for a premium feature that comes in by default without the customer needing it.

Where can we see a comparison between the enhanced edition and the Premium edition.

entry.title: Re: Internal credit/debt for credit or debit memo requests
entry.content: Instead of letting the IDoc just post the debit/credit note in Italy without creating the internal invoice, you could enhance the IDoc processing logic (via BAdI or custom mapping) so that when a special credit/debit is sent, it automatically triggers the creation of an internal invoice/credit in the receiving company. That way, your customer gets the same internal invoice/credit consistency as in the normal sales/returns process, without burdening users with manual steps.

entry.title: Re: STO Process Intercompany After Outbound Delivery Dire...
entry.content:
Hello @Ashish13

You cannot “fix” the standard intercompany STO process to keep MIRO and remove billing in Public Cloud. In 5HP Advanced Intercompany Stock Transfer, billing and invoice verification is part of the standard flow as mentioned earlier.

So, your customers only realistic options are:

Option 1: Keep intercompany STO standard (STO, outbound delivery, transportation charges, GR, intercompany billing, supplier invoice/MIRO). This is the only standard route if they want MIRO in an intercompany STO.

Option 2: Change the business design. If your customer does not want billing, then do not use 5HP as the target process. Instead, use a stock transfer with delivery style process and manage the transportation charges separately in TM. Note that STO integration with TM can handle transportation planning and freight cost settlement. But in design, you should not expect MIRO against an intercompany supplier invoice, because that belongs to the 5HP billing flow.

So, the direct recommendation you can give to your customer is:

  • If they insist the want MIRO, then billing must stay.
  • If they insist on no billing, then they must accept o non 5HP stock transfer design, with freight costs handled separately and without the standard intercompany MIRO chain.

Hope this provides clarity.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SAP BTP ABAP Environment – Pre-Upgrade Option for Release 2605
entry.content:
Overview

Providing a significant number of new features is an important goal of release upgrades to deliver innovations to our customers and partners. There are four major release upgrades per year following a predefined schedule as described in this blog post.

An upgrade is intended as a “no event” for customers and partners. For example, only compatible changes of released objects (APIs) will be delivered. We recommend performing a test of the custom-built productive apps in a test environment for the new release. The complexity of the technical dependencies between the custom-built apps and the underlying environment cannot be tested isolated, only including the ABAP environment on SAP-side. Therefore, we offer you to pre-upgrade selected systems 29 days in advance to test the applications prior to the planned standard-upgrade.   

 

Purpose

This option enables a customer or partner to test the applications they have already developed on top of the new release a couple of days before the standard-upgrade. With SAP BTP ABAP Environment (aka "Steampunk"), we follow the approach of keeping released objects stable and provide all enhancements in a compatible fashion. Early testing is an additional measure to ensure proper software quality and might be used to detect unforeseen regressions. However, this option is not intended to test new features or to consume new features earlier.

 

Approach

The standard-upgrade of all systems to release 2605 is planned for May 16/17.

A customer or partner can nominate one or more already existing systems for the pre-upgrade. A nominated system will be upgraded on April 17 within a planned downtime from 8:00 to 16:00 CET. Depending on the system settings, the actual technical downtime might be only ~20 min within the mentioned timeframe. Please refer to this blog post about the downtime-optimized upgrade. 

We recommend nominating a non-development system, such as a test system. The nomination of a development system is technically also possible, but fixes or other changes must not be imported into another system with a lower release. The nomination of a productive system doesn’t make sense because the purpose of the pre-upgrade is the re-test of the existing application before the productive system gets the new release.

According to the approach explained above, this offer is only relevant for customers or partners having at least two systems.

There might be situations where a correction of custom objects needs to be deployed. Such corrections are possible if the development / correction system is on the older release and is imported into the pre-upgraded system with the higher release.

Except potential fixes, the pre-upgrade is based on the same version as the standard-upgrade. It is planned to perform the pre-upgrade based on hotfix collection 1. The pre-upgrade systems will get the import of hotfix collection 2 after 2 weeks in the middle of the entire pre-upgrade test phase. It is planned to perform the standard-upgrade based on hotfix collection 3 and import this hotfix collection 3 to the pre-upgrade systems on May 16/17 as well.

 

Costs

This offering is optional and free of any additional charges.

 

Planned New Features

The planned new features will be published on the What's New for SAP BTP ABAP Environment on April 17. This list gives you an overview of potential areas for re-testing. The purpose of the pre-upgrade is not to consume these features before the standard-upgrade, because the documentation on help.sap.com will be delivered later before the standard-upgrade. It would also not be possible to transport code of such an adoption, because subsequent systems are not yet on the new release during the pre-upgrade phase.

Independent of the upgrades of the SAP BTP ABAP Environment, we recommend developing automated tests for the custom-built apps to detect regressions early on and without manual effort for re-testing. Such automated tests are perfectly suited to be run in the pre-upgrade test phase.

 

Time Schedule

The following figure summarizes the general approach:

 

Milestones for release 2605:

  • March 17: Nomination of system(s) is possible in the Landscape Portal
  • April 13: Announcement of the planned new features
  • April 13: Pre-upgrade systems are known
  • April 17: Pre-upgrade (with hotfix collection 1)
  • April 30: Import of hotfix collection 2 into pre-upgrade systems
  • May 13: Potential decision to postpone the upgrade
  • May 16/17: Standard-upgrade (with hotfix collection 3)

 

System Nomination

To nominate a system, please register the system(s) by April 13 (EOB) latest by using the Landscape Portal. You can find further information about Accessing the Landscape Portal and about Register Systems for Pre-Upgrade. Please do not create customer tickets for system nomination like in previous pre-upgrades.

With such a nomination, the customer agrees to an extraordinary planned maintenance on April 17 for the pre-upgrade and on April 30 for hotfix collection import (see above).

 

Report an Issue

Issues should be reported as part of the regression test phase by Mai 1 (EOB) to allow for a sufficient time frame for issue analysis and potential corrections on SAP-side.

Please create a customer ticket by using component BC-CP-ABA.

If a detected issue is likely to endanger the productive use after the standard-upgrade, you should use ticket priority very high despite the fact that the pre-upgrade system is not a productive system.

 

Exceptional Process in Case of Unresolved Priority Very High Issues

In case of the aligned decision on Wednesday, May 13 (based on unresolved priority very high tickets) to postpone the upgrade, a dedicated mitigation process needs to be started together with the respective customer or partner, with the following boundary conditions:

  • Resolving the open priority very high issues remains the highest priority for both, SAP and the customer or partner, as it is also the prerequisite for the upgrade.
  • According to the estimated issue resolution, catching up the upgrade needs to be planned.
  • The upgrade leads to a business downtime of 6 – 8 hours.
  • The upgrade must be scheduled within 7 calendar days the latest after fixing all remaining open priority very high issues.
  • We will not provide any fixes for the old release after the original date of the standard-upgrade to the new release.

In case business-critical issues are detected after the upgrade:

  • Issues need to be reported as priority very high issues and will be processed accordingly.
  • Going back to the release before upgrade is not an option. Therefore, resolving the issues in the new release with the appropriate priority is the required approach.

 

Summary

We recommend performing regression tests in pre-upgraded test systems to avoid day-1 impacts of productive custom apps after the standard-upgrade.

entry.title: Deep Dive: Essential Setup for S/4HANA and SAP Field Service Management Native Integration
entry.content:
Introduction

In today's fast-paced service landscape, the seamless flow of data between back-end ERP systems and front-line service operations is critical. The native integration between SAP S/4HANA and SAP Field Service Management (FSM) unlocks significant business value by enabling real-time master data synchronization, unified asset management, and end-to-end service order processing. By connecting these systems, organizations reduce manual data entry efforts, minimize errors, and ensure that field technicians have accurate, up-to-date information on equipment, customers, and spare parts.

This blog provides a deep technical walkthrough of the essential configuration steps required to set up this native integration. We will cover the complete landscape, starting from connectivity setup with Cloud Connector and authentication via OA2C, to configuring the Data Replication Framework (DRF), monitoring via the Application Interface Framework (AIF), setting up the Mobile Application Interface Framework (MAIF), and finalizing the FSM connector settings. Whether you are a technical consultant or an integration architect, this guide serves as a comprehensive reference for your implementation journey.

Integration Architecture Overview

The native integration leverages the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) as the integration middleware, enabling secure, bidirectional data flow between the on-premise S/4HANA system and the cloud-based Field Service Management solution. This architecture ensures robust connectivity through secure tunnels and utilizes standard, pre-built integration content.

Figure 1: Overview

Section 1: OA2C Configuration

OAuth 2.0 Client Credential Setup

This configuration is used for OAuth 2.0 client credential authentication setup between S/4HANA and SAP BTP services. It is a mandatory prerequisite before proceeding with other configurations.

Transaction Code: OA2C_CONFIG

Figure 2: OA2C Configuration page with FSM_PROFILE

Figure 3: Configure FSM_PROFILE with Client ID and Client Secret

Section 2: RFC Configuration

RFC (Remote Function Call) setup is the backbone of backend communication within the SAP landscape. Ensure that the RFC destinations are correctly configured to allow for seamless data exchange.

Figure 4: Create RFC of Type G

 

Figure 5: Enter the host name in RFC

Figure 6: Assign the OAuth Profile created in Section 1, to RFC

 Figure 7: Set the SSE Client PSE ID as Anonym SSL Client

Section 3: Cloud Connector Setup

The Cloud Connector serves as the secure tunnel between your on-premise S/4HANA system and the SAP BTP environment.

Access Link: https://<host>.cpi.c.eu-de-1.cloud.sap:8443/

  1. Log in to the Cloud Connector administration portal.
  2. Select Cloud to On-Premise from the menu to establish the tunnel mapping from SAP BTP to your S/4HANA backend system.
  3. Configure the internal host and port mapping to the virtual host and port.

Figure 8: Assign your cloud Connector in BTP

Figure 9: Cloud Connector Login Page

 

Figure 10: Select the BTP Sub Account

Figure 11: Configure Cloud to On-Premise with Hist Name and Authentication

Security Tip: 

Ensure that the system certificates are correctly exchanged between the Cloud Connector and the S/4HANA backend to prevent handshake errors during connectivity.

Section 4: SOAMANAGER Configuration

Transaction: SOAMANAGER

This configuration is critical for the setup of web services and is required for the following business objects:

  • Business Partner (BP)
  • BP Relationship
  • Product
  • Product Price
  • Product Stock

Figure 12: List of Objects that needs to be configured in SOAMANAGER

Configuration Steps:

  1. Create Manual Logical Port definitions for the consumer proxies.
  2. Set the newly created port as DEFAULT.
  3. Maintain technical user credentials for authentication.
  4. In the Transport Settings tab, enter the URL obtained after the iFlow deployment. This ensures that BP/SOAP events are correctly routed to Cloud Integration.

Figure 13: SOAMANAGER Home Screen: Select Web Service Configuration

Figure 14: In this example – Business Partner is configured

Figure 15: Create Manual Define logical ports and make the port as DEFAULT

 

Figure 16: Enter the Logical Port name

Figure 17: Enter technical user id for Authentication

Figure 18: Maintain the Messaging Tab as shown

Figure 19: Transport settings – mention the URL of obtained after iflow deployment

Note on iFlow URL

The iFlow URL must be obtained after deploying the relevant Integration Flow on SAP BTP Integration Suite. Configure this step after the endpoint is available.

Figure 20: End point URL of the IFLow after deployment

Section 5: Data Replication Framework (DRF)

DRF is the central tool used to distribute master and transactional data from S/4HANA to SAP FSM. It allows for flexible configuration of replication models and filters.

 Key Transactions

T-CodePurpose
DRFIMGCustomizing transaction to define replication models
DRFOUTTriggers the actual data replication (manual run)
DRFLOGViews logs to monitor data transfer status

 

Configuration Steps

  1. Define Business Systems: Navigate to create a new entry for the FSM target system

Figure 21: Define Business System

  1. Define Business Objects: Configure objects like Business Object 986 (Business Partner) and define what data entities should be replicated.

Figure 22: Define Business System

  1. Define Communication Channel: Set the Communication Channel to Replication via services.

Figure 23: Define Communication Channel

 

  1. Define Replication Models: Create a replication model that links the Business Object to the Target System.

Path - Data Replication -> Define Custom Settings for Data Replication -> Define Replication Models

  • Assign Outbound Implementation: Assign the relevant outbound implementation class to the model.

 

Figure 24: Assign Outbound Implementation

  • Assign Target Systems: Link the replication model to the specific FSM business system defined in step 1.

Figure 25: Assign Target Systems for Replication Model

  • Assign Outbound Parameters: Configure any additional parameters required for the replication logic.

Figure 26: Assign Outbound Parameter

  1. Activate the Replication Model: Finally, activate the model to enable live replication.

 

Figure 27: Activate Replication Model

 Testing Replication

Use T-Code   DRFOUT to manually trigger replication for testing purposes before scheduling background jobs. Use DRFLOG to inspect the results.

Section 6: AIF – SAP Application Interface Framework Monitoring

SAP AIF is used to monitor and manage integration messages between S/4HANA and FSM, providing a user-friendly interface for error handling.

Content Extraction

T-Code: /n /AIF/CONTENT_EXTRACT

 Extract the AIF content using the following scenario IDs:

Scenario IDIntegration Scope
SAP_AIF_0102Service Integration
SAP_AIF_0107Plant Maintenance Integration

Figure 28: Extract AIF Content

Viewing Loaded Content

Transaction Code - /n /AIF/CONTENT_DISP - Use this to view the loaded AIF Content after extraction.

Figure 29: Display extracted AIF Content

NOTE

Both SAP_AIF_01 02 and SAP_AIF_01 07 must be activated depending on your specific integration scope (Service vs. Plant Maintenance). Failure to activate the correct content will result in unmonitored messages.

 

Section 7: Customizing Configuration Values

This step involves maintaining specific configuration tables that control the behavior of the integration.

  • Maintain configuration tables with API details (Client ID, Client Secret, Auth URL).
  • Maintain DTO (Data Transfer Object) version settings to ensure compatibility between S/4HANA and FSM versions.
  • Save the configuration.

Figure 30: Integration Customizing Parameters

Figure 31: Maintain the parameter values as mentioned in help doc as a customizing entry

Section 8: Client Proxy Framework & Event Configuration

bgRFC Queue Setup

 Create bgRFC queues manually using T-Code SBGRFCCONF.  These queues are essential for handling background RFC processing for outbound events, ensuring that user transactions are not delayed by integration calls.

 

Figure 32 Maintain the bgRFC inbound destination manually

Section 9: Service Order Event Activation

Activate events in T-Code SWE2 . This configuration links the business object events (e.g., Service Order Created, Changed) to the receiver function modules that trigger the replication to FSM.

Figure 33: Activate events in SWE2

Troubleshooting Tip

Ensure all required event types are active in SWE2 before testing end-to-end replication. Missing event activations are a common root cause for failed replications where data changes in S/4HANA do not trigger any outbound message.

Section 10: MAIF – Mobile Application Interface Framework

MAIF is the framework required for replicating technical asset data. It is specifically used for the following object types:

  • Equipment
  • Functional Location
  • Characteristics
  • Classification

Configuration Steps:

  1. Activate BC Set: Use Transaction code SCPR20 to activate the BC Set /ACI/ASSET_CENTRAL_INTEGRATION and CRMS4_FSM_INTEGRATION_REF.
  2. Verify MDO Configuration: Navigate to Tcode -/n/SYCLO/CONFIGPANEL and verify that the Master Data Object (MDO) entries exist for the asset objects.Figure 34: Check if BC Set is activated
  3. Admin & Monitoring: - Use T-Code /n/SYCLO/ADMIN to maintain to maintain integration parameter details like API details, DTO versions etc.

    Figure 35: Maintain integration parameters in Additional Properties of Server Management

    Figure 36: Check Log in Inbound transaction Monitoring

Section 11: FSM Connector Settings (FSM → S/4HANA Inbound)

FSM Admin Portal

URL: https://<Cluster>.fsm.cloud.sap/admin

Routing iFlow

Configure the Routing iFlow in SAP BTP Integration Suite. This iFlow acts as the entry point from FSM to CPI to receive FSM event payloads. Events triggered in FSM (e.g., service confirmation, time recording) are received here and routed back to S/4HANA.

Figure 37: FSM Connector Configuration

Figure 38: Additional Configurations for different Objects

Error Monitoring & Reprocessing

Use the FSM Connector Error Monitoring tool for tracking failed inbound messages. This interface allows administrators to view error details and reprocess failed messages directly from the monitoring console.

Figure 39: FSM Connector Error Monitoring and Reprocessing

Conclusion

 

This blog covered the complete technical setup landscape for native S/4HANA-FSM integration, from authentication and connectivity (OA2C, Cloud Connector) through data replication frameworks (DRF, MAIF) to monitoring tools (AIF). Following these configuration steps ensures a robust, production-ready integration that supports seamless data consistency across your service operations.

As next steps, we recommend testing end-to-end scenarios—from master data replication to service order execution—while monitoring the data flow using AIF and FSM connector tools. Additionally, always keep your integration content updated by leveraging SAP's continuous updates to the BTP Integration Suite packages.

References & Useful Links

  • SAP Help Portal – Field Service Management Integration

entry.title: Rescheduling of maintenance orders after breakdown
entry.content:
Hi!

I've got a question about rescheduling orders after a breakdown.

Let's say we have the following scheduling, calendar day based, cycle is 3 months.

As we can see, the called order is scheduled for 03.19, and this is the same day the next order gets called as the call horizon is 0%.

Let's say we have a breakdown of this part on 02.10, so maintenance gets carried out. Now, we obviously dont want to perform the task on 03.19., so we change the basic start-finish dates of the maintenance order in transaction IW38 to approx. 05.10.

Is this the correct procedure, and will this change the call dates of the following orders aswell? Also, is this procedure correct for counter based maintenance aswell?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Poland Tax Repporting date correction process with KSEF
entry.content: There will be no zero invoice - there will be minus correction for the full value of the original invoice and then the new invoice, again with the full value. So in the process, there will be 3 documents: invoice -> correction -> invoice; and all 3 should be send to KSEF and shown in JPK. This process should be discussed and confirmed with business users in Poland. SAP issued such a recommendation because the system cannot deal with non-value corrections. But it is not correct in Poland to fully credit every invoice in order to be able to correct every minor issue.

thanks!

maria

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI - json value characater gets escaped when convert to xml
entry.content: You cannot because it is done to avoid the generation of invalid XML which would be the case if the & character were not escaped.

entry.content:
@EmilianoAyub @wagener-mark

As is -   200 + EDI Partners <-> MS Biztalk  <-> SAP ECC

To be - 200 + EDI Partners <-> MS Biztalk  <-> SAP S4 - Utilize same enchanced IDOC to minizmei code changes in biztalk for faster delivery
