Thankyou!!

entry.title: 1010 Balance Carryforward - Divestured Consolidation Unit's Reserves are not BCF
entry.content:
After consolidation unit has been divested, its reserves are maintained because they represent realized profits that increased the group's equity and, upon consolidation, became part of consolidated equity, regardless of the fact that the subsidiary no longer belongs to the group.

But the BCF Balance Carryforward task at SAP GR Data Monitor task is not taking into account these reserves at the beginning balance of the following year.

Are we missing any any automatic task or setup that manages this point?

If the answer is no, please would you mind to let us know which are SAP best practices for this point.

entry.title: Is there any costing validation at process order creation before save?
entry.content:
Hope everyone aware that we can set the erro message on process/ Production order if there are costing issue. 

1. Error message "Profit Center not maintained Order xxx” - often message KO 438 – OBA5, OBMSG 
2. Error messages in the costing log “No price could be determined for a material /batch xxx plant yyy” CK 465 - OPR4_PPC

3. Reject release if the status Costed with Errors (CSER)? BS22
But this validation is not working at creation stage COR1/CO01.  Consider I create a process order and first release it and then save it. The above validation is based on costing. But the costing is done only at saving the order. By the time I have released the order. Is there any validation which either do the costing / set the status before release on CR01/CO01 transaction.


May say process order to be converted from planned order. But in case of rework order how it will work?

entry.title: Using local (Belgian) G/L account numbers vs. YCOA: what’s the exact rule across apps?
entry.content:
Hi all,

We run S/4HANA Public Cloud with YCOA as the operational CoA. We want to work with local (Belgian) G/L numbers for day‑to‑day use (at least for entry/display), while accepting YCOA as the technical posting account.

My understanding:

  • In FI apps where users manually select a G/L, local/alternative accounts can be used for selection/display (e.g., Post General Journal Entries via Edit Options for Journal Entries → Alternative Account).
  • In system-determined scenarios (MM-driven postings, AP/AR operational apps, uploads/APIs), postings run on YCOA; local numbers may appear in value help but are not the operational posting account.
  • Example: In Create Supplier Invoice, Offsetting Account value help shows both local and YCOA numbers, but after selection the app posts/displays YCOA.

Questions:
Could you please provide a concise list or rule-of-thumb matrix of where local/alternative accounts are usable (entry/display) and where they are not in Public Cloud—beyond Post General Journal Entries (e.g., accruals, reclassification, asset postings without PO, etc.)? Representative examples are sufficient; no need for an exhaustive tile-by-tile list.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba Invoicing (formerly SAP Ariba Central Invoi...
entry.content:
Yes, from the 2602 (Feb 14th) release, SAP Ariba Invoicing (formerly SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management), SKU 8018575, is available for our S/4HANA Private Cloud, On Prem (2022 sp1 or later), SAP ERP (ECC 6 EHP 8 or better) customers (in addition to S/4HANA Public Cloud).
The same SKU and functionality applies to all SAP ERP's. SAP Ariba Invoicing should be positioned as a central service, connected to one or many SAP ERP's (of the same or different types). The metric is total #of invoices processed and replicated from the ERP in blocks of 1,000 invoices/year. Min block size is 20. 

Note: there may be some regional limitations in regards to availability in the price list. Please go to the DE or US pricelist if you cannot find it. We've asked pricing to update to availability in alignment with localizations available (30+ to date).

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1 is now available.

To access the final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer. In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access it.

For you as a customer, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool. The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.1 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

You can use the commenting feature for What’s New. With this feature, authors can evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.
